faq.question: How do I apply for a long-term course?
faq.answer: Visit the Long-term Courses page and contact our admissions team. We will schedule an assessment and guide you through the enrollment process.

faq.question: What are the class timings?
faq.answer: Class timings vary by course. Long-term programs run in morning and evening sessions, while short-term courses are scheduled on weekends or evenings. Check each course page for details.

faq.question: Do you provide separate classes for sisters?
faq.answer: Yes. The Momina program and women's workshops are conducted by female instructors with dedicated classrooms.

faq.question: Is there a placement assessment?
faq.answer: Yes, students may take a short assessment in Quran recitation and basic Islamic knowledge to ensure the right placement.

faq.question: Are certificates provided after completion?
faq.answer: Students receive certificates for both long-term and short-term courses. Advanced students may pursue ijazah pathways under faculty supervision.

faq.question: Can working professionals join short-term courses?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Short-term courses are designed for working adults and families with flexible schedules.

faq.question: Do you offer scholarships or fee support?
faq.answer: Limited fee concessions are available based on need. Please speak with the admissions office for eligibility details.

faq.question: How can parents support Hifz students?
faq.answer: Parents are encouraged to supervise daily revision, maintain the progress log, and attend monthly feedback meetings with instructors.
